rulings.Case Title: CREDITOR TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT VS. BLUE SKY FAMILY CLUB LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answers To Post Judgment Discovery Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on on Tuesday, July 8, 2014, Line 1: PLAINTIFF CREDITOR TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT WESTERN COLLECTION BUREAU'S Motion To Compel Answers To Post Judgment Discovery Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel answers to Post-Judgment Discovery is granted and was unopposed. Defendant shall respond to plaintiff's interrogatories and document request within 30 days of the date of service of this Order with Notice of Entry. Plaintiff shall re-serve the aforesaid discovery pleadings dated October 17, 2013 simultaneously with service of the Order. Monetary sanctions are awarded to the moving party in the sum of $300.00 Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC., VS. PARMINDER S. CHAHAL INDIVIDUALLY DBA MONTEREY MINI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 8, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT PARMINDER S. CHAHAL INDIVIDUALLY DBA MONTEREY MINIMART's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Motion denied. Defendant failed to present any evidence, argument, or explanation as to why Plaintiff's service of summons did not conform to the statutory requirements for service of process. Moreover, Defendant plainly had actual knowledge of the action prior to entry of the Default Judgment, and has failed to explain the delay in bringing this Motion. Finally, on the grounds of mistake, inadvertence, surprise and excusable neglect the Motion is untimely. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW V. SMITH VS. THE OPERA PLAZA RESIDENTIAL PARCEL HOMEOWNERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Deeming Requests For Admissions And Requests For Admissions As To Genuineness Of Documents Admitted And For Order Assessing Sanctions Against Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on on Tuesday, July 8, 2014, Line 2: DEFENDANTS THE OPERA PLAZA RESIDENTIAL PARCEL HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION'S and THE OPERA PLAZA MASTER OWNER'S ASSOCIATION'S ("MOA") Motion For Order Deeming Requests For Admissions And Requests For Admissions As To Genuineness Of Documents Admitted And For Order Assessing Sanctions Against Plaintiff. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's Motion to Compel Responses to Form Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One; Request for Production of Documents, Set One, is granted and was unopposed. Verified responses to said discovery, without objection, shall be served within 30 days of service of a copy of this Order with Notice of Entry. Defendant's Motion to Deem Requests for Admissions, Set Two, Admitted, is conditionally denied or granted. Court records reflect on June 20, 2014 the plaintiff who was In Pro Per retained counsel and a Substitution of Counsel was filed. Defendant's motion is denied if verified responses to the Request for Admissions and Form Interrogatory 17.1, both without objection, are served within 30 days from the date of service of this Order with Notice of Entry. Otherwise the motion is granted. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,100.00 are awarded as against plaintiff only. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RICCI CORNELL VS. CORZAC INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Staying Enforcement Of Order Appointing Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 8, 2014, Line 6, PLAINTIFF RICCI CORNELL'S Motion For Order Staying Enforcement Of Order Appointing Receiver. Granted. The February 4, 2014 Order appointing a receiver is stayed pending appeal. CCP sec. 917.5 is not discretionary and therefore the order must be stayed, provided Plaintiff posts the undertaking. The timeliness of Plaintiff's appeal is determined by the appellate court. (See Cal. Rule of Court 8.104, "If a notice of appeal is filed late, the reviewing court must dismiss the appeal.") The stay will take effect upon Plaintiff posting an undertaking in the amount of $50,000. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: HELEN L MARSH VS. DANIEL L FEDER
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 8, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANT DANIEL FEDER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The court overrules the demurrer to the 6th cause of action for violation of Labor 203 [waiting time penalties]. Paragraphs. 28 and 29 of the complaint sufficiently allege that the condition precedent (settlement of class action and payment to defendant) occurred and plaintiff is entitled to payment. Discovery will reveal the precise dates that payment became due. The court sustains the demurrer to the negligent misrepresentation cause of action without leave to amend. The misrepresentation that defendant would pay plaintiff a certain sum relates to a future event and is not actionable. "To be actionable, a negligent misrepresentation must ordinarily be as to past or existing material facts. '[P]redictions as to future events, or statements as to future action by some third party, are deemed opinions, and not actionable fraud.'" Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153, 158. The court overrules the demurrer to the UCL cause of action under Cortez v. Purolator Air Filtration Products Co. (2000) 23 Cal.4th 163, 173. That case clearly holds that a party can recover unlawfully withheld wages as restitution under the UCL. The court overrules the demurrer to the promissory estoppel cause of action because defendant's moving papers address a different claim. The court overrules the demurrer to the declaratory relief cause of action. Plaintiff is seeking prospective relief because some of the class actions that plaintiff worked on are still settling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
